title: Datasets
subtitle: Datasets within the project (visible to members, writable by Contributor+)

fromRegistry.title: Data to dataset
fromRegistry.subtitle: Pick fields from "{{type}}" project records, mask sensitive ones, and generate an AI-ready dataset.
fromRegistry.maskedHint: Sensitive fields (🔒) are masked/excluded by default.
fromRegistry.rawSensitiveHint: Includes plaintext smiles/mol/seq; requires Manager + e-signature.
fromRegistry.refModeTip: Reference columns (e.g. HEAVY/LIGHT/LINKER) store the referenced record's uuid. Options: (1) Record name (e.g. "Seq-005", a readable label); (2) Actual content (the referenced record's main content field — sequence string, compound SMILES — best for analysis; but these are sensitive fields, so you must also enable "Export raw sensitive fields" to get plaintext, otherwise it falls back to the name); (3) Business id (the id field); (4) Raw uuid (precise, joinable).
fromRegistry.refModeContentMaskedHint: Actual content is a sensitive field and is currently masked → falls back to the record name. To export plaintext, also enable "Export raw sensitive fields" above (requires e-signature).
fromRegistry.rawSensitiveTip: By default the dataset excludes sensitive fields (masked). Turn this on to also export plaintext smiles/mol/sequence — a high-sensitivity action, Manager-only, requiring an e-signature for traceability. AI training usually doesn't need plaintext structures, so keep it off.
fromRegistry.esignLoginHint: Defaults to your display name; fill in if your WeMol login differs
fromRegistry.esignLoginTip: WeMol SSO accounts re-authenticate the e-signature with your WeMol username + password. Leave blank to try your display name; the system verifies the WeMol account is really you and rejects a mismatch.
fromRegistry.esignHint: Exporting raw sensitive data requires an approved e-signature (21 CFR Part 11)
fromRegistry.esignTip: 21 CFR Part 11 compliance: high-risk actions like exporting sensitive plaintext require re-entering your login password as an e-signature. The signer, time and meaning are recorded immutably.
fromRegistry.needEsign: Exporting raw sensitive fields requires a valid approved e-signature for this asset type. Re-enter your password and try again.

lineage.title: Lineage
lineage.desc: derived_from sources of this dataset (source types and records).
lineage.empty: No lineage (not generated from registry data).

public.title: Public datasets
public.subtitle: Platform-maintained datasets, read-only across all companies — preview and export.
public.emptyDesc: Once a platform admin uploads them, available public datasets appear here.

create.title: New dataset
create.name: Name
create.fileHint: Optional. If selected, the dataset is created and the file is uploaded as its first version.
create.uploadCsvDescription: Choose CSV files or a CSV folder. Files create datasets by file name; a folder creates one dataset by folder name.
create.uploadCsvFileHint: Choose one or more CSV files. Each CSV creates one dataset using the file name.
create.uploadCsvFolderHint: Create one dataset using the folder name, then upload CSV files inside as versions.

empty.title: No datasets yet
empty.description: Two ways: ① click "New dataset" to upload a CSV/Excel; ② easier — go to Drug Assets and click "To dataset" to turn entered records into a masked dataset in one click.

toast.createdWithFile: Dataset created and first version uploaded

versions.title: Versions
versions.empty: No versions yet. Upload a CSV or Excel file.
versions.rolesHint: Label each column's role for AI training: feature = input, label = prediction target, id = identifier (not trained on), ignore = skip. Once set, the dataset is ready to feed a model.

preview.title: Preview
preview.empty: No data. Upload a version first.
preview.footer: Showing {{shown}} / {{total}} rows · click a header to sort · column roles feed the AI training pipeline.

collab.managedInSettings: Resource-level collaboration grants are managed in project settings.
